content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN with 0≤Ai<K0 \le A_i < K0≤Ai​<K.

Consider performing the following operation any number of times on it (possibly zero):


Choose i,x,yi, x, yi,x,y such that 1≤i<N,0≤x,y<K1 \le i < N, 0 \le x, y < K1≤i<N,0≤x,y<K.
Insert z=((x⋅Ai+y⋅Ai+1)mod  K)z = ((x \cdot A_i + y \cdot A_{i + 1}) \mod K)z=((x⋅Ai​+y⋅Ai+1​)modK) just before the iii-th element, and then delete AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1​.
Formally, the array is changed to A[1,i−1]+[z]+A[i+2,N]A[1, i - 1] + [z] + A[i + 2, N]A[1,i−1]+[z]+A[i+2,N] and A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subarray [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​].
Count the number of arrays that can be obtained from AAA by performing the above operation any number of times. Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK, the size of the array and the upper bound.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of arrays that can be formed modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The arrays that can be formed are [0,1],[0][0, 1], [0][0,1],[0] and [1][1][1].

We can choose to do no operations to get [0,1][0, 1][0,1].
We can choose to do an operation with i=1,x=0,y=0i = 1, x = 0, y = 0i=1,x=0,y=0 to get [0][0][0].
We can choose to do an operation with i=1,x=1,y=1i = 1, x = 1, y = 1i=1,x=1,y=1 to get [1][1][1].
